case_name: Elick v. Christina Care Health Services, Inc.

summary.brief: Elick v. Christina Care Health Services, Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: On August 11, 2019, while under Defendants’ care, Grala was diagnosed with both a pseudoaneurysm in his right common femoral artery and a large deep vein thrombosis
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Evans decided to treat the pseudoaneurysm prior to treating the DVT and on August 12, 2019, performed a thrombin injection as Grala’s initial treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Grala collapsed and died at 1:31 a.m. on the morning of August 13 prior to treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Hicks may testify that, even untreated, the risk of Grala developing a pulmonary embolism was low during the relevant time frame
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The basis for any statistical conclusion stemming from this study is irrelevant under a D.R.E. 402 analysis.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Even assuming this study and basis of the statistical evidence holds relevance, it cannot survive a D.R.E. 403 analysis, as its probative value is outweighed by its prejudicial effect.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient diagnosed with a pseudoaneurysm in the right common femoral artery and a large deep vein thrombosis (DVT) adjacent to the pseudoaneurysm.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Vascular Surgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Evans regarding treatment plan for pseudoaneurysm and DVT.

reveal.result_summary: Decision to treat pseudoaneurysm first with thrombin injection.
reveal.clinical_significance: Prioritization of pseudoaneurysm treatment over DVT.

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with pseudoaneurysm and DVT.

timeline_phases.name: Treatment Planning
timeline_phases.description: Decision made to treat pseudoaneurysm first.

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient diagnosed with pseudoaneurysm and DVT.
decision_points.prompt: What should be the treatment priority?

options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate treatment of DVT to prevent pulmonary embolism.

options.description: Treat pseudoaneurysm first with thrombin injection.
options.clinical_reasoning: Prioritized pseudoaneurysm treatment, delaying DVT management.

expected_action_court.description: DVT should have been treated first.
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Immediate anticoagulation for DVT to prevent complications.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to treat DVT promptly increased risk of pulmonary embolism.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: DVT should have been managed with anticoagulation immediately.

patient_outcome.description: Patient collapsed and died from a pulmonary embolism.

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to treat DVT promptly.
Causation established - untreated DVT led to fatal pulmonary embolism.

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to treat DVT with anticoagulation.
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely anticoagulation could have prevented pulmonary embolism.

factual_timeline.event: Patient diagnosed with pseudoaneurysm and DVT.
Patient collapsed and died from pulmonary embolism.
